03-REQ-033.note: HTTP API authorized by bearer API keys; admin-only creation (per resolved 05-REVIEW-004), revocable. The revocation means retires with 05-REQ-052 onto key-management.

03-REQ-034.note: Unrecoverable (one-way digest) storage; plaintext disclosed exactly once at creation. Dedupe cluster with 05-REQ-046 and 08-REQ-095b/095c; hash algorithm and key format are mechanism.

03-REQ-035.note: Key bound permanently to the creating admin; global scope. Dedupe with 05-REQ-047; the scope's consistency with the strictly observational admin role is carried by key-capability-bounds (see design.md).

03-REQ-036.note: Keys never authorize identity creation or actions requiring interactive OAuth. Dedupe with 05-REQ-048.

05-REQ-045.note: Bearer-key authorization of every HTTP API request; reject missing/invalid/revoked/creator-not-admin (per resolved 05-REVIEW-004). The 'expose an HTTP API' half retires with 05-REQ-049 onto http-api-surface.

05-REQ-046.note: Persistent per-key record: one-way digest, label, creating admin's user record, creation timestamp, null-until-revoked revocation timestamp; plaintext never stored or exposed after the single disclosure.

05-REQ-047.note: Global, never scoped-down key scope. 'Equivalent to the admin role' deliberately not restated verbatim: the mutating reach is bounded to the API surface by key-capability-bounds to stay consistent with identity-and-authorization/platform-admin-role's read-only extension (design.md).

05-REQ-048.note: No endpoints for identity creation, OAuth interactions, direct game-access-token issuance, or Centaur-subsystem state.

05-REQ-049.note: Endpoint families as behavioural scope statements (teams, rooms, game read/start, webhooks, keys); URL shapes and payload schemas are mechanism per the author decision.

05-REQ-050.note: Authored generically per the matrix note: API mutations obey the same invariants as the first-party surface; the legacy roster-freeze example is not cited (that rule lives with its owning capability and binds through parity).

05-REQ-051.note: Creation means through the first-party application and the API itself, admin-only; the exactly-once plaintext disclosure half retires with 03-REQ-034 onto key-custody.

05-REQ-052.note: Revocation means; all subsequent requests with the revoked key rejected, immediately (constraint-mined: no grace or cache window; revoked records retained per #revoked-records-retained).

05-REQ-053.note: Subscription registration by API-key-authenticated callers; captures URL, event types from the closed {game_start, game_end} set, game-or-room scope, owning key, creation timestamp.

05-REQ-054.note: game_start on the playing transition (game-lifecycle/status-authority) to matching subscriptions; payload includes game id, room id, configuration (launch-frozen).

05-REQ-055.note: game_end on the finished transition to matching subscriptions; payload includes game id, room id, final scores. Covers walkovers via status-authority's not-started -> finished transition (design.md).

05-REQ-056.note: At-least-once with exponential backoff and a bounded retry budget; stable dedup identifier in every notification (constraint-mined stability: determined solely by game/event-type/subscription; the {gameId}:{eventType}:{webhookId} format is mechanism).

05-REQ-057.note: Delivery never blocks lifecycle transitions, replay persistence, or teardown (game-lifecycle/teardown-after-persistence cited). Kept local per the matrix note rather than promoted to global-invariants.

05-REQ-058.note: Subscriptions auto-revoked with their owning key; no deliveries for events occurring after revocation.

08-REQ-095a.note: The management surface listing active and revoked keys with label and timestamps. 'Every authenticated user' superseded by the admin-only resolution of 05-REVIEW-004 — re-authored as an admin affordance (design.md).

08-REQ-095b.note: Single creation-time plaintext display with an explicit copy affordance before dismissal.

08-REQ-095c.note: Never display/store/transmit plaintext after the single disclosure — deduped with the identity capability's custody hygiene by citing identity-and-authorization/client-credential-custody (whose one-time-disclosure exception this is) rather than restating it.

08-REQ-095d.note: Scope communication at the management surface, specialized to the admin-only model: a key carries its creator's global admin authority and stops working if the creator loses the role (per-request re-check in admin-api-keys#creator-no-longer-admin).

05-REVIEW-005.note: Option A: only game_start, firing at the not-started -> playing transition. No game_created or game_will_start event — subscribers first hear of a game at launch, when its configuration is frozen and actionable (#start-payload-is-the-played-config carries the rationale behaviourally).
